stem: Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish the student's goal?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Urban parks increase property values and provide recreational opportunities, while trees filter air pollution and residents report higher life satisfaction in cities with more green space.

choices.label: B
choices.text: By reducing cardiovascular disease rates, lowering stress levels, and encouraging physical activity, urban green spaces function as essential public health infrastructure that simultaneously strengthens community well-being and individual wellness.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Research shows that urban parks offer multiple benefits: they reduce air pollution, provide exercise opportunities, improve mental health, and increase property values by 8-15%.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A 2019 study found that people living near parks had 15% lower cardiovascular disease rates, and cities with more green space report higher life satisfaction, demonstrating the health benefits of urban parks.

explanation: Choice B most effectively accomplishes the student's goal by synthesizing the health-focused information from the notes into a cohesive argument that emphasizes parks as 'essential public health infrastructure.' It strategically combines the most relevant evidence (cardiovascular benefits, stress reduction, physical activity) while using sophisticated language ('function as,' 'simultaneously') that reinforces the dual benefit to individuals and communities. The choice maintains focus on the public health angle while demonstrating the broader community impact.

distractorRationale.A: This choice lists benefits but lacks focus on the public health emphasis the student wants. It includes property values prominently, which dilutes the health argument, and doesn't effectively synthesize the information into a cohesive public health framework.
distractorRationale.C: While this choice mentions health benefits, it presents them as a simple list rather than synthesizing them into the 'crucial public health intervention' framework the student seeks. The inclusion of property values as equally weighted undermines the health focus.
distractorRationale.D: This choice focuses too narrowly on specific statistics rather than synthesizing the broader public health argument. It mentions only two benefits and doesn't effectively combine them into the comprehensive public health intervention concept the student wants to emphasize.

passage:
While researching the impact of urban green spaces on public health, a student has gathered the following notes:

• A 2019 study of 8 major cities found that residents living within 300 meters of parks had 15% lower rates of cardiovascular disease.
• Urban trees can reduce air pollution by filtering particulates and absorbing carbon dioxide.
• Green spaces provide opportunities for physical activity, such as walking, jogging, and recreational sports.
• Mental health benefits include reduced stress and anxiety levels among park users.
• Property values typically increase by 8-15% in neighborhoods adjacent to well-maintained parks.
• Cities with more green space per capita report higher overall life satisfaction scores among residents.

The student wants to write a report emphasizing how urban parks serve as a crucial public health intervention that benefits both individuals and communities.

passageMetadata.source: Student research notes on urban green spaces
